---
title: "Module 11 Assignment"
description: |
  The Future of Web Technologies
preview: https://github.com/skywalshUSF/LIS4365_Blog/blob/main/_posts/2026-03-12-module-10-assignment/studentTable.png?raw=true
author:
  - name: Skylar Walsh
date: 2026-04-01
output:
  distill::distill_article:
    self_contained: false
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

## Here are my responses to our module 11 tasks

### Step 1

Post any questions you may have about the Final Project and I will
answer them. Also, if you have found a clever solution to a problem,
please feel free to share.

Here is a link to a copy of my module 11 discussion forum post on my
website:

<https://skylarwalshlis4365.infinityfree.me/Module11.html>

### My Discussion Forum Post

For my final project, I built Tampa Paws Rescue, a full-stack site that
brings everything we have tackled this semester in LIS 4365 together.
Specifically, all of the skills we learned working with HTML, PHP, and
MySQL. The goal of our final project was to create a functional,
database-driven platform to promote a local non-profit, which handles
real-world tasks like dynamic content loading, CRUD, and secure data
entry. I spent a lot of time in the wireframing phase to make sure the
layout made sense for a rescue organization before I started working on
the code. And now, I am focusing on how well that plan translated to the
final version. As I wrap things up, I would like to get your opinion on
a few technical spots where I am trying to polish up my final draft.

1.  When working on the PHP/MySQL connection, do you have any tips for
    structuring queries so the "Available Pets" gallery pulls data as
    efficiently as possible?

2.  I have implemented some prepared statements for input from the web
    forms, but I am curious if there are any specific PHP
    techniques/methods for extra validation to keep the database secure?

3.  When looking at how my final HTML/PHP compares to the original
    wireframe, how can I keep the hierarchy intact when adding dynamic
    elements?

4.  Regarding the CRUD requirements, I have already built the logic to
    add, remove, and trigger updates to the database; do you think the
    final project also needs a dedicated "edit/modify" feature for
    existing records?
